precondition: Fresh app startup or a normal returning user opens ReiLink
expected_behavior: The default product surface is the main chat workspace with Rei status and input available, not Event Stream, Prompt Preview, Raw JSON, or a large Debug workspace.

precondition: UI Surface v0 workspace shell is available
input: Click a left navigation item such as Memory, Game, Voice, Overlay, Settings, or Developer
expected_behavior: The left navigation behaves as a workspace launcher that opens a focused module surface rather than only scrolling to a section in one crowded right column.

precondition: A user wants to inspect or confirm memory
expected_behavior: Memory has a normal user-facing entry with Pending, Confirmed, Ignored, Search, Sources, and future Session Archive placement. It is not hidden only inside Debug.

precondition: A user wants to inspect the current game context
expected_behavior: Game has a normal user-facing entry for current game, boss, session state, knowledge support, supported games, and manual control without requiring the Debug workspace.

precondition: A user wants to configure Voice Input or Voice Output
expected_behavior: Voice has a first-class entry with Conversation placeholder, Input / Local ASR, Output, and Voice Profile placeholder instead of being buried only in Settings.

precondition: The Voice module is presented in docs or UI Surface v0 copy
input: Read Voice planning or open Voice workspace
expected_behavior: Voice clearly distinguishes current transcript-first Local ASR from the future direct spoken conversation loop with idle, listening, transcribing, ready_to_send, assistant_thinking, speaking, interrupted, and error states.

precondition: A user opens Overlay planning or Overlay workspace on macOS
expected_behavior: Overlay shows Safe Mode boundaries: macOS auto-show is intentionally fail-closed, force close remains available, and the feature is not described as a complete game HUD.

precondition: A developer or QA user wants diagnostics
expected_behavior: Event Stream, Prompt Preview, Semantic Shadow trace, Knowledge trace, Persona Pack summary, Runtime status, and safe Raw JSON live under Developer / Debug and do not overwhelm ordinary users by default.

Prompt Preview remains a safe summary surface with persona id, version, loaded sections, missing sections, fallback, truncation, raw_content_omitted, and path_omitted; it does not show full persona markdown or full prompt.

precondition: A user opens Memory or Game after a gameplay session
expected_behavior: Confirmed long-term memory, candidate memory, and ignored memory are distinct from current game session state, boss state, death count, frustration count, and session timeline.

precondition: Future Presentation / Avatar appears in the IA or UI Surface v0
expected_behavior: Live2D / Avatar is clearly a future presentation layer and placeholder. It does not become the current main experience and should follow Voice, Overlay, Memory, and Debug split work.

precondition: UI Surface v0 lets users switch between Home, Memory, Game, Voice, Overlay, Settings, and Developer
input: Type text in chat, switch panels, then return to Home / Chat
expected_behavior: Workspace switching preserves unsent chat input and does not reset current conversation state, Voice state, Overlay setting, Memory confirmation state, or Game Context.

precondition: UI Surface v0 has separate Voice, Overlay, Memory, Game, and Developer workspaces
expected_behavior: Settings focuses on app-level preferences, model/persona mode, privacy, local data, reset controls, and safe app configuration. Feature-specific deep setup moves to each module workspace where possible.
